metadata.title:
SIGNS Up
ALL BUT TWO


SHOPS

pages.raw_text:
With the exception of NiXon-Kim-
nell and WarrenOllver, every cleo
trical contractor in the city signed
the Spokane Building Trades ALI
ance agreement with the union
electrical workers, between $50
and 10 O'Clock this morning. Close
silence on both sides has been
maintained for the past few days
regarding the deadlock which has
workers and the contractors since
May 1.


This mornings developments
practically ends the fight and vir-
tually in favor of the union. The 2
remaining unsigned shops will en-
deavor to maintain an open shop.
They declare that they will not sign
even though the others have broken
the ice.


None of the strikers have suffered
by the prolonged lockout. as all
have been amply supplied with
work in the few shops which signed
almost at first notice, but they are
all visibly pleased at their success
in winning the fight.


The union has held itself willing
to confer with the contractors upon
any occasion and have offered at
any time to call an open meeting
of their members that the contrac-
tors might be permitted to come
face to face with the workmen
themselves, instead of a committee
from the body. Thus the fight has
been waged and now that it has
been ended so nicely no hard feel
